title: IDF Strikes Kill Several in Gaza; Israeli Soldier Seriously Hurt in Operational Accident
detail: Israeli forces conducted multiple strikes in Gaza killing several Palestinians as daily operations continued despite the October 2025 ceasefire. Simultaneously, an IDF soldier was seriously wounded in an 'operational accident' inside Gaza — marking a notable non-combat Israeli casualty. The ongoing strikes brought the total number of Palestinians killed since the October 10, 2025 ceasefire to at least 750, according to Gaza's Ministry of Health. UN Human Rights Chief Volker Türk called the 'unrelenting pattern of killings' a reflection of 'continuing disregard for Palestinian lives, enabled by sweeping impunity.' Gaza's Government Media Office has documented over 2,400 Israeli ceasefire violations since October 2025.

media.caption: IDF strikes continue in Gaza as Israeli soldier wounded in operational accident

title: Palestinian FM: Iran War 'Distracting World from Dire Conditions in Gaza'
detail: Palestinian Foreign Minister Varsen Aghabekian, speaking at the Antalya Diplomacy Forum in Turkey, warned that the US-Israel war on Iran has diverted global attention from the humanitarian catastrophe in Gaza. She described conditions: 'People in Gaza are living not only on rubble, but on tons of trash, and the rats are eating the fingers, the ears of children, men and women, and the world is watching.' She said the shift to the Iran war had produced 'more suffering' and 'more delays' in getting humanitarian aid to Gazans. Her statement came as UN OHCHR confirmed at least 32 Palestinians had been killed by Israeli forces since early April 2026 alone.

media.caption: Palestinian FM warns Iran war distracting world from Gaza's dire humanitarian conditions

title: Israeli Settlers Pepper-Spray Palestinian Farmers Near Halhoul; IDF Blocks Land Access
detail: Israeli settlers pepper-sprayed Palestinian farmers working their land near Halhoul village, south of Hebron in the occupied West Bank. Israeli security forces subsequently stopped Palestinians from accessing their farmland after the settler attack. The incident occurred amid a documented surge in settler violence in the southern West Bank, with over 1,700 incidents documented in one reporting period — up from 1,400 the previous period — and settlers killing 8 Palestinians in the first three months of 2026 compared to 7 in all of 2025.

media.caption: Settler attacks and access restrictions continue to escalate in southern West Bank
